title: Tracey Emin’s Cult of the Self
description: The YBA artist spearheaded contemporary art's trend of coupling extreme self-introspection with relentless self-promotion.
hook: Tracey Emin turned her messiest impulses into contemporary art's most profitable brand. How did self-exposure become self-promotion?

title: Natalie Marlin on The Chemical Brothers’ Hanna OST
description:
In this month's Low Culture Essay, Natalie Marlin explores how a under-regarded soundtrack became part of understanding her evolving trans identity

Hanna does not live with music to guide her, at least at first. Her life is scored solely by the openness of the tundra, the crackling of fire. The stillness of open air, of onyx-drenched night. A dwelling share
hook: A Chemical Brothers soundtrack became the unexpected mirror for understanding her own trans identity. How film scores rewire us.

title: Álvaro Enrigue on “Now I Surrender”
description:
“Books just grow like trees. You cannot control them. They become something different than what you thought it was.”

The post Álvaro Enrigue on “Now I Surrender” appeared first on Public Books.
hook: A novelist explains why books escape their creators' intentions entirely—the uncomfortable truth about what we actually write.

title: Lucy Sante on collage and the elimination of possibilities
description: The writer and artist discusses her visual work, on view at New York's American Academy of Arts and Letters
hook: Lucy Sante on collage as refusal: how eliminating possibilities on canvas mirrors the artist's relationship to language and choice.

title: Field Notes from a Body
description: "As I watched I had felt a nameless churn in my gut, some tremor of feeling I couldn’t describe."
hook: A visceral essay about bodily experience that captures the unnamed churn of feeling before language can pin it down.
